title: artemisia campestris var. adscendens
intro: Artemisia campestris var. adscendens, also known as field wormwood, is a plant species within the Artemisia genus. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its volatile oils, including camphor and borneol. The health benefits of field wormwood include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate headaches, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Field wormwood can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
